description: A colon-place is INVALID after `set ... to` (spec/grammar.md:104 requires a bare-place): `set :x to 100` fails immediately because the token after `set` must be a bare name, not `:x`. Error recovery then re-parses `:x` as its own statement, flags the following `to` as a run-on (missing terminator) and then as an unreadable token, producing three parse-stage ol-bad-token diagnostics — the deterministic, verified recovery cascade for this rejection, not a bug.
